mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 5% signals a tightening labor market that increases the cost of labor for firms, leading to reduced corporate profitability and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism's direction flips unexpectedly during a recession.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production below 100 signals a weakening demand environment that reduces corporate revenue and profitability, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in weakening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism's direction flips unexpectedly during a recession.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in housing starts above 1.2 million signals an increase in supply that reduces the scarcity premium in housing markets, leading to reduced corporate profitability and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in supply-increasing periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism's direction flips unexpectedly during a recession.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the federal funds rate above 4% signals a tightening monetary cycle that increases discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism's direction flips unexpectedly during a recession.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in initial jobless claims below 250 thousand signals a strengthening labor market that increases the cost of labor for firms, leading to reduced corporate profitability and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in strengthening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism's direction flips unexpectedly during a recession.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in real GDP above 3% signals a strengthening demand environment that increases corporate revenue and profitability, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in strengthening periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism's direction flips unexpectedly during a recession.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the personal savings rate below 5% signals a decrease in supply that increases the scarcity premium in housing markets, leading to increased corporate profitability and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in supply-decreasing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism's direction flips unexpectedly during a recession.
